Job Title: Strategic Analyst -  Disability Insurance & Executive Benefits
Job Description: The Strategic Analyst, Disability Insurance & Executive Benefits, supports strategy execution and initiative governance by translating strategic direction into clear plans, prioritized initiatives, and leadership‑ready insights. This role partners cross‑functionally with Product, Finance, Legal, Distribution, Marketing, and Technology to coordinate planning activities, track progress, and ensure alignment between enterprise strategy, DI and Multi‑Life priorities, and funded initiatives. The analyst maintains strategy roadmaps and initiative portfolios, prepares executive materials and dashboards, documents decisions and trade‑offs, and monitors risks, dependencies, and performance metrics to enable informed leadership decisions and disciplined execution. You are A structured, detail‑oriented business analyst who helps leaders turn strategy into action. You are comfortable working across multiple teams, pulling together data, assumptions, and inputs to support planning, prioritization, and execution. You communicate clearly, document decisions carefully, and bring discipline to tracking initiatives, risks, and outcomes. You are equally effective in preparing executive ready materials and managing the day‑to‑day coordination needed to keep complex initiatives moving forward. You have Professional Experience: 3-5 years of relevant experience in business analysis, strategy execution, or program and initiative management, including experience in insurance, finance, or operations roles. Experience supporting strategic initiatives in a matrixed organization. Financial services or insurance experience preferred: especially with Disability and/or Executive Benefits. Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent work experience. Technical Experience Ability to build and maintain executive ready dashboards, updates on portfolio Leadership: Demonstrates strategic thinking by translating enterprise and business‑line strategy into clear priorities, plans, and decision insights. Leads through influence without authority to align cross‑functional teams and drive outcomes. Communicate effectively with senior leaders by distilling complex information into clear, concise, and actionable messages. Applies strong organizational discipline to bring structure, rigor, and follow‑through to planning and execution. Strategic & Analytical Thinking Ability to assess strategic options, trade‑offs, and risks, and translate complex inputs into clear recommendations that support leadership decision‑making. Strong analytical skills to interpret data, monitor initiative performance, and identify insights, trends, and implications across a portfolio of strategic initiatives. You will Prepare executive‑ready materials for strategy, planning, prioritization, and governance discussions. Compile and synthesize data, assumptions, and cross‑functional inputs to support strategic decision‑making. Coordinate agendas, pre‑reads, meetings, and follow‑ups for strategy and prioritization forums. Maintain strategy roadmaps, priority lists, and a portfolio view of active initiatives. Track initiative timelines, milestones, owners, risks, dependencies, and alignment to objectives and capital assumptions. Consolidate status updates into concise summaries and dashboards for senior leadership. Document decisions, approvals, scope or funding changes, and key action items. Support business cases, capital requests, and investment trade‑off analysis through clear insights and recommendations. Reporting Relationships As our Business Analyst DI and Executive Benefits, you will report to our Head of Multi-life Risk Strategy & Underwriting and DI&ML Portfolio Management Location Bethlehem, PA, Holmdel, NJ, or Pittsfield, MA with work from home flexibility. Travel 20% of travel is required for this role for in-person and/or sales meetings. Salary Range: $68,970.00 - $113,310.00 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. The salary for this position ultimately will be determined based on the education, experience, knowledge, and abilities of the successful candidate. In addition to salary, this role may also be eligible for annual, sales, or other incentive compensation. Job Description: We are seeking a strategic problem-solver for Guardian’s Enterprise Strategy team. In the role of Enterprise Strategy Manager , you will be part of a project team that solves complex problems, develops long-range and short-term plans for Guardian, and partners with senior leaders to identify, develop, evaluate, prioritize, and deliver strategic initiatives across the organization.  You will work closely with Guardian’s Strategic Projects and Corporate Development teams, as well as senior leaders in the business units and corporate functions. The role reports to Guardian’s Head of Strategic Growth & Planning.  This highly visible team is responsible for overall strategy development, executing cross-cutting strategic initiatives, and managing strategic planning processes across the enterprise. You will have the opportunity to work on a range of high-visibility projects and strategic workstreams spanning across: Strategy development – help define the strategic direction, with both near-term and long-term priorities for Guardian or specific business units.  You will do this using research, analytics, logical problem solving, and strategic frameworks. Strategic planning and monitoring – translate long-term strategic priorities into operational strategic planning processes, including the setting of Objectives & Key Results and the allocation of portfolio resources toward the most high-value projects.  Monitor cascading metrics and goals for business areas aligned with strategic plan. Execution of cross-cutting strategic initiatives – support cross-enterprise strategic efforts that are critical to Guardian’s overall success.  Support in managing the business performance of the initiatives (for example, implement and manage processes to monitor business performance metrics aligned with the strategic plan). Strategic communications development – support the development and delivery of content for Guardian Leadership Team and Board of Directors, with a focus on strategic recommendations, progress, and priorities. New initiative exploration – in collaboration with business areas, assess and develop business cases, market sizing, and external research to drive new ideas on innovation, partnerships, and areas of investment outside BAU. To successfully deliver on these projects and workstreams, you will: Utilize strategic frameworks to structure problem-solving and identify opportunities or issues Structure and build quantitative and qualitative analyses of operational and financial data Perform competitive and industry research Identify implications of data and analysis Develop concise written communication materials for senior management and present your findings in key meetings and working groups Build trust with colleagues across Guardian You are: An excellent generalist problem-solver who can break large, meaty questions into their component parts and use data, logic, and reason to come to a solution or recommendation A strong analyst with proficiency in Excel and quantitative problem solving, in addition to qualitative research An effective communicator (both written and oral) A collaborative team player who can work effectively in cross-functional settings with business partners A proactive and resourceful self-starter who can work independently and take initiative Extremely organized; able to prioritize multiple tasks and competing deadlines Detail oriented Intellectually curious You have: Bachelor’s degree from a top institution with strong academic performance 5+ years of experience in roles related to strategy consulting or in-house strategy teams; experience at a top management consulting firm preferred but not required Strong analytical toolkit; advanced proficiency with Microsoft Excel Strong written and verbal communication skills; proficiency with Microsoft PowerPoint You will: Have exposure to, and play a role in shaping, the agendas of our key stakeholders Develop expertise in a variety of functions and businesses across the company Be mentored and coached by experienced leaders on problem solving, communications, and leadership skills Location: This role is Hybrid with 3 days a week in our Hudson Yards NY location Salary Range: $118,980.00 - $195,465.00 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. Job Title: Workday Technical Lead, Payroll, Time & Absence
Job Description: Position Summary The Workday Technical Lead – Payroll, Time & Absence is responsible for the end-to-end technical ownership, design, and governance of Workday Payroll, Time Tracking, and Absence modules. This role serves as the system authority for the domain, ensuring scalable design, data integrity, and alignment with business and compliance requirements. This position operates within IT and partners closely with Payroll, HR, and external vendors to deliver reliable, efficient, and compliant solutions. While not responsible for payroll processing, this role is accountable for the systems and configurations that enable accurate payroll outcomes. Key Responsibilities Domain Ownership & Technical Leadership Own the architectural and configuration guardrails for Payroll, Time, and Absence, coordinating execution while retaining accountability for outcomes. Establish and maintain standards for configuration, data integrity, and system governance. Make and guide key decisions on system design, scalability, and long-term architecture. Serve as the primary escalation point for complex system and payroll-impacting issues. System Design & Configuration Lead configuration of payroll elements, time tracking rules, and absence plans with a focus on scalability and maintainability. Ensure configurations support business requirements while minimizing downstream risk. Evaluate and redesign existing configurations to improve performance, accuracy, and usability. Payroll Systems Enablement (U.S.) Ensure Workday Payroll configurations support accurate and compliant payroll processing. Partner with Payroll leadership to identify risks, gaps, and optimization opportunities. Own enterprise‑level resolution of payroll‑impacting issues, including root cause identification, risk mitigation, and long‑term remediation. Global Payroll Integration (India – External Vendor) Own design and reliability of outbound payroll data integrations. Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and auditability across systems. Coordinate with external vendors to resolve systemic issues and improve processes. Architecture, Integrations & Data Own architectural decisions and trade‑offs for payroll, time, and absence integrations, balancing compliance, scalability, and operational risk (e.g., EIB, APIs, vendor feeds) Ensure alignment between Workday and downstream/upstream systems Define and maintain data standards, controls, and validation processes Workday Release & Change Governance Lead impact assessment and strategy for Workday biannual releases Establish testing standards and ensure high-quality deployment of changes Govern system changes to ensure stability and avoid unintended impacts Continuous Improvement & Strategy Identify opportunities to simplify processes, reduce manual work, and improve system efficiency Translate business needs into scalable, forward-looking system solutions Shape and influence the broader HR technology roadmap, representing Payroll, Time, and Absence priorities and trade‑offs. Stakeholder Leadership Act as the primary technical advisor and decision partner to Payroll, HR, and IT stakeholders Provide clear recommendations and guidance on system capabilities and limitations Influence decision-making through expertise and data-driven insights Required Qualifications 5+ years of Workday experience with deep expertise in: Workday Payroll (U.S.) Time Tracking Absence Proven experience owning system design and configuration decisions Strong understanding of payroll processes, compliance, and downstream impacts Experience supporting integrations and data flows across systems Demonstrated ability to lead complex problem-solving and root cause analysis Preferred Qualifications Workday certifications in Payroll, Time Tracking, or Absence Experience with global payroll integrations and vendor coordination Experience with Workday integrations (EIB, Studio, APIs) Background in IT or HR technology environments Success Factors Operates as the accountable owner of the domain, not just a contributor Makes sound technical decisions with long-term impact in mind Balances stability with continuous improvement Anticipates risks and proactively addresses them Communicates complex technical concepts clearly to stakeholders Location : Three days a week at a Guardian office in New York, NY, Holmdel, NJ, Stamford, CT or Bethlehem, PA. Job Title: Workday Platform & Governance Owner - Workday SME
Job Description: Position Summary The Workday Platform & Governance Owner serves as the technical subject matter expert (SME) and governance authority for the Workday ecosystem supporting 10,000+ employees. This role is responsible for tenant management, security administration, production change control, configuration standards, and complex cross-module configuration within Workday. This position operates within IT, operating within a structured HRIS (functional design) and HRIT (technical delivery) model.  This role ensures Workday remains secure, architecturally aligned, compliant, and technically optimized. While HRIT Engineers own module delivery, this role serves as the enterprise technical SME across all Workday domains and provides hands-on configuration for complex or cross-functional initiatives. Key Responsibilities Enterprise Technical SME (All Workday Modules) Serve as the senior technical authority across: Core HCM Position Management & Job Management Benefits Compensation & Advanced Compensation Recruiting Talent Management Workday Learning Payroll Time Tracking & Absence Security Integrations (EIB, Core Connectors, APIs, Studio fundamentals) Responsibilities include advising HRIT engineers on complex configuration design, new releases, AI agents and new functionality, validating cross-module impacts, supporting troubleshooting of high-severity issues, and mentoring team members on advanced configuration practices. Hands-On Configuration Responsibilities Configure complex cross-domain business processes Design and implement advanced security groups and domain policies Configure complex eligibility rules and condition logic Support advanced compensation cycle setup Configure complex Position Management structures Build enterprise-impact calculated fields Support integration troubleshooting Perform configuration clean-up and technical debt remediation Tenant Management & Environment Governance Create and Maintain Tenant Management Plan Oversee all Workday tenants (Production, Sandbox, Dev, Preview) Coordinate sandbox refresh schedules Enforce Dev → Test → Prod migration controls Approve production configuration migrations Oversee release preview cycles Maintain production access governance Support emergency change management Lead governance processes, including prioritization of enhancements, upgrades and releases. Establish and enforce technical and application change management standards, ensuring compliance with documentation and audit requirements Security & Access Governance Maintain enterprise Workday security model Monitor and remediate segregation of duties (SOD) conflicts Conduct periodic security reviews Review elevated access requests Partner with IT Security and Audit teams Production Change Control & CAB Leadership Chair Workday Change Advisory Board (CAB) for Intake and Demand Review cross-module impact of proposed changes Validate testing documentation prior to approval Maintain structured change control records Required Qualifications 7–10 years HRIS experience 5+ years advanced hands-on Workday configuration Workday Pro Certifications Deep expertise across all major Workday modules Strong Position Management and Job Management experience Experience designing and maintaining security architecture Strong understanding of SOD and audit controls Experience operating within a multi-tenant governance model Location: Three days a week at a Guardian office in New York, NY, Holmdel, NJ or Stanford, CT> Salary Range: $152,290.00 - $250,195.00 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. Job Title: Marketing Manager, Enrollment and Service Marketing
Job Description: Marketing Manager, Enrollment and Service Marketing Come join Guardian’s team of innovative and collaborative marketing professionals. If you're ready to challenge yourself with work that matters, then this is the place for you. We're committed to making employee benefits insurance simple to understand, choose and use – and we have a blast doing it! Is a culture where “We believe people count,” “We do the right thing,” “We courageously shape our future together,” and “We go above and beyond for the people we serve” important to you? If so, Guardian is seeking an experienced, results-focused, and energetic marketing manager to join our Member Engagement and Service Marketing Team for the Group Employee Benefits business. This role will collaborate closely across marketing, product, enrollment, claims and client management teams to develop and execute omnichannel marketing strategies that enable our members to better understand and use their benefits. Based on this, the successful candidate will develop and implement results-oriented marketing plans executed across a variety of marketing channels, to drive create/use customer journey mapping to help enhance the customer experience. This role will establish KPIs and goals for campaign programs and communicate the results to key business stakeholders. This role requires strategic thinking, in a fast-paced environment, business acumen, collaboration, prioritization, attention to detail, a passion for our customers, and commitment to diversity and inclusion. You are: A successful direct-to- consumer, B2C or B2B2C Marketer who thrives in a fast-paced, time-sensitive environment.  You are well-organized and have a collaborative and flexible personality who adapts easily to change. Able to drive alignment between marketing, product, claims and client management to further business growth, and build relationships to accomplish work across business functions. Experience with translating business objectives and customer insights into marketing campaigns with proven results against goals. Experienced with developing campaign hypotheses, analyzing campaign data, and coming to conclusions quickly to keep programs moving forward toward goal. Capable of understanding digital solutions and their impact on a customer experience, and adept at explaining their benefit to a variety of internal and external audiences. Comfortable with subject matter experts, executives, and peers. You can collaborate across departments to transform conceptual thinking into executable insights and inform customer segmentation opportunities and create useful sales enablement tools. A strong communicator (written and verbal) and has strong executive presence. You have : Education: Bachelor’s degree Experience: Minimum of 5 years of direct to consumer, B2C/B2B2C working with business to develop campaigns and resources designed to enhance the customer experience Strong track record of developing and executing multi-channel digital marketing programs with tangible results. Experience with customer segmentation. Presentation skills that include creating engagement with stakeholders and sharing technical content at appropriate audience levels Proven collaboration across cross-functional teams at the rep and management levels Experience with marketing automation platforms, prospecting databases, and knowledge of web analytics tools, social media management and social selling platforms Demonstrated willingness to ‘roll up your sleeves to get things done Strong analytical skills and the ability to identify trends and make data-driven decisions Prior experience in managing consumer-focused marketing campaigns in the healthcare / insurance industry or financial services Strong understanding of life and disability insurance/ leave planning solutions a plus Account-Based Marketing (ABM) experience with proven results against goals  and experience with ABM platforms (like Demand Base) a plus MS Office (Excel, Word, PPT) You will: Develop and implement results-oriented marketing plans executed across a variety of marketing channels, including digital and account-based marketing, to drive member engagement. Es tablish KPIs and goals for campaign programs and communicate the results to key business stakeholders. Ensure successful ROI -focused planning, implementation, and execution of marketing plans across the full range of marketing channels (web, email, paid media, webinar) Think strategically to provide marketing leadership to d evelop and execute omnichannel campaigns that achieve the business’s short and long-term goals, and generate buy-in and support for marketing programs from the sales team Ensure fast, clear communication of all relevant marketing activities and tools to relevant stakeholders, and collect regular feedback Identify, develop, implement, and improve customer enablement tools, programs, and training to support member engagement and digital adoption to decrease costs, improve productivity and achieve business results Inform and train internal teams, distribution teams, and customer-facing staff on marketing programs and planholder enablement tools Understand the customer journey from end-to-end. Bring original thinking in innovating new ways to reach, engage and convert target customers Measure and report performance for marketing campaigns and assess against goals (ROI and KPIs) and drive ongoing data-driven insights Be a strong storyteller …. Create new ideas/approaches to effectively enhance the communication of key messages in sales presentations Salary Range: $95,170.00 - $156,355.00 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. Job Title: Group Benefits Sales Representative, Premier Market - Oregon
Job Description: As a Premier Sales Representative for The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America in Oregon, you will drive new business development, cultivate client relationships, and strategically manage the sales pipeline to achieve ambitious sales targets within the Oregon market. Success will be determined based on the ability to attain high levels of production through broker development and internal relationships. This role will report to the Market Leader within the assigned territory. You will: Market, position, and sell Guardian’s entire Group Benefits portfolio products and technology solutions to your designated brokers. This includes Dental, Vision, Life, Disability, Absence, and Supplemental Health products. Support the general wellbeing of the broker and client relationship for the development and growth of Guardian sales and client retention. Understand territory dynamics to build, develop and maintain broker relationships to grow the book of business while representing Guardian’s vision to inspire well-being. Be accountable for consistently achieving or exceeding personal sales production expectations by having a clear understanding of products and creating and implementing tactical sales strategies. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to examine market trends and stay ahead of consumer demands. Represent Guardian by acting as a market leader, sharing thought leadership, and maintaining strong community relationships. Job Description: Every day, Guardian helps our 29 million customers realize their dreams through a range of insurance and financial products and services. Our Purpose, to inspire well-being, guides our dedication to the colleagues, consumers, and communities we serve. We know that people count, and we go above and beyond to prepare them for the life they want to live, focusing on their overall well-being — mind, body, and wallet. As one of the largest mutual insurance companies, we put our customers first. Behind every bright future is a Guardian. Learn more about Guardian at guardianlife.com. Guardian has set out an ambitious growth strategy over the next 5 years, referred to as our Journey To ’30.  This strategy focuses on continuing the growth of businesses by diversifying our product portfolio, increasing our distribution footprint, growing our customer base, and leveraging technology, data and AI as a strategic enabler. This growth strategy requires ongoing transformation across the enterprise, including both the business lines and corporate functions. Guardian is seeking a highly analytical, execution-oriented individual to serve as Corporate Development Manager . In this high-impact role, you will help drive Guardian’s inorganic growth initiatives and help our businesses forge innovative and transformative partnerships across the enterprise. Job Title: Head of Individual Markets Business Risk & Resiliency
Job Description: As the Head of Risk & Resiliency, you will execute the Risk & Resiliency frameworks for Financial Protection & Retirement Solutions (FPRS) and Client Solutions & Wealth Management (CSWM), Guardian’s Individual Markets lines of business. You will manage the operational risk profile for FPRS and CSWM, aggregating risk across domains (Technology, Cyber, Data, Model, Compliance, Third Party, etc) and ensuring alignment to Guardian’s risk appetite and tolerance. You will enable informed business decision‑making by providing oversight, independent challenge, and proactive risk insights, supported by risk domain experts within Enterprise Risk Management (ERM). You will Act as a trusted advisor to FPRS and CSWM Leadership to drive the Risk & Resiliency program according to Guardian standards, in part by facilitating Risk Working Groups on a regular cadence for transparency and accountability. Escalate topics to the appropriate risk committees as warranted. Assess key risks at the Line of Business and process level and ensure visibility into key risk action items through coordination and engagement with business process owners and risk and control partners. Execute the Business Resiliency framework through development of business continuity plans and resiliency solutions for critical dependencies, regular testing, and oversight of plans, and managing business coordination of Disaster Recovery and Crisis Management efforts. Own and drive maintenance of the Risk Register (ServiceNow) for FPRS and CSWM, including establishing reporting standards, promoting self‑identification of risks, and ensuring data quality and completeness. Oversee consolidation and synthesis of risk data across all domains to maintain an aggregated residual risk profile, assess alignment to risk appetite, identify material risk themes and trends, and inform LOB leadership and risk governance discussions. Coordinate and integrate risk activities, including issues, events, Key Risk Indicators(KRIs), and stress scenario analysis, ensuring appropriate linkage, escalation, and consistency across domains. Drive a risk‑aware culture, enabling first‑line ownership of risk and partnering with Business Risk Coordinators to promote consistent standards, effective issue identification, and timely escalation. You  are A confident, visible leader and trusted advisor comfortable operating in a highly transparent environment, engaging senior leaders, risk committees, and cross‑functional stakeholders to support informed business decision‑making. A change leader with strong business acumen who provides clear risk insights, credible challenges, and pragmatic trade‑off perspectives to advance business priorities. An exceptional communicator and collaborative relationship builder with a proven ability to partner across lines of business, risk domains, and enterprise functions to drive outcomes through others. You have 5+ years of experience in operational risk management or a related risk discipline within the financial services industry, insurance experience preferred. Strong analytical, decision making and problem-solving skills. Flexibility and resourcefulness in managing multiple priorities. The ability to accelerate impact and the desire to lead change. Reporting relationship This position is an individual contributor reporting to the Head of Business Risk & Resiliency, who reports to our Chief Risk Officer within Enterprise Risk Management. Location: Hybrid role - 3 days in a Guardian office located in Hudson Yards, NYC; Holmdel, NJ; Stamford CT; Bethlehem PA; Boston, MA or Pittsfield, MA. 2 days WFH. Job Description: Position Summary Guardian’s Annuity New Business CARE team seeks a dynamic and knowledgeable individual to join our team as an Annuity Relationship Manager . We are looking for someone with exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and intellectual curiosity; enabling us to discover deeper insights into our business and identify innovative ways to accelerate growth. This individual will be responsible for supporting all facets of annuity new business administration and working with internal and external partners. As a member of our CARE Team, you are part of a dynamic group focused on achieving team goals and identifying ways to improve consumer experience.  As the Annuity Relationship Manager, you’re highly motivated and empowered to take ownership and accountability for each consumer interaction, keep consumers informed during every step of handling their request, demonstrate genuine care, and actively engage with consumers to create meaningful and unexpected experiences. CARE expresses the emotion we want to generate, and it’s also an acronym for Communicate, Advocate, Respond, and Empower – the actions needed to show CARE. You are Highly adaptable and comfortable with change and ambiguity. Someone with a growth mindset who fosters collaboration and a positive culture. Goal-oriented and able to deliver results aligned with business priorities and key performance metrics. Highly focused on the consumer, motivated to do whatever it takes to make a positive impact and deliver personalized experiences. Able to handle several responsibilities, multiple phone calls and field interactions, yet be able to identify and focus on the most valued added phone support and use of time. An independent decision-maker able to utilize resources and logic to solve problems quickly with minimal leadership assistance. Confident and skilled to control consumer and field escalations; able to utilize resources and use logic to solve problems quickly. You will Serve as a subject matter expert on annuities, demonstrating a comprehensive understanding of various annuity products, features, and regulations. Effectively cover incoming and outgoing phone calls, serving as a central point of contact for our field partners. Meet and exceed individual performance metrics including average calls per day, adhering to daily schedule, Quality Assurance call scores, consumer survey scores, etc. Serve as a primary point of contact for field partners, ensuring all requests, inquiries, and communications are handled promptly and professionally. Partner with Product Management, Compliance, and various departments across the organization to orchestrate successful and compliant product launches. Support day-to-day team activities and needs including covering roles in times of absence or increased new business volumes. Operate at a high level to consistently exceed quality goals and service level agreement standards. You have 6+ years’ experience in the financial service industry. Experience must include direct customer service experience. A bachelor’s degree or equivalent industry experience. FINRA Series 6, preferred. In-depth knowledge of various annuity products, including fixed, variable, and indexed annuities. Excellent phone-based skills, articulate, polite, confident, able to establish relationships with the Field. Experience in field training and presenting to small/large groups via Zoom/Teams. A consumer-centric mindset; you know what it takes to deliver personalized experiences. Exceptional written and verbal communication skills. Location This role is remote with possible travel to a Guardian Life hub for stakeholder meetings. Job Title: Distribution Strategy Market Management Lead - Group Benefits
Job Description: Guardian's Group Benefits Market Management Team is looking for a Distribution Strategy Market Management Lead to help support and advance this well-established and growing team! In this role, you will work closely with our field sales, client management, underwriting, pricing, and other production areas to enable Guardian’s Group Benefits business to achieve its strategic goals by formulating and executing on strategies to win in market. As the Distribution Strategy Market Management Lead, you will be accountable for: coordinating and developing localized market deep dives and assessments to understand regional performance drivers and collaborate with Distribution, Underwriting, Product, Pricing, and Marketing to develop and execute on strategies to achieve business objectives and growth goals within those markets. Additionally, you will be responsible for identifying and executing on new and unique growth opportunities to bolster sales. You will also continue to enhance the go-forward framework  to ensure Guardian is capitalizing on insights, trends, and growth opportunities in the sales planning process. Additionally, you will use data and analytics to understand historical performance to unlock localized growth opportunities within our Distribution organization. You will report to the Head of Market Management which is part of the broader Distribution organization. You are: A self-starter who has a passion for cross-functional collaboration and executing on business strategies Passionate about using quantitative analysis to uncover trends and insights that inform data-driven strategy and decision-making, and skilled at communicating these findings to influence key stakeholders and leadership An insightful strategic thinker, capable of developing outside the box and unique solutions to complex business business Able to apply and adapt corporate strategies to the needs of business segments and partnerships An individual with a strong business acumen and excellence in collaboration, presentation, project leadership, change management, communications, coaching and strategic planning Unafraid to challenge the status quo to ensure Guardian is achieving its business objectives You have: 7+ years of Group Insurance experience (required) with experience in Distribution or Distribution Strategy, Underwriting, Pricing, and/or Product Management Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience; Masters degree preferred but not required Expert knowledge of Group Insurance Distribution, Underwriting, and/or Product operating models, with advanced knowledge of overall Group Insurance market dynamics A strong background in leveraging large, disparate datasets to derive quantitative analysis aimed at uncovering localized trends and insights, and partnering with field leaders to develop and execute on strategies to achieve business goals Ability to lead large cross functional initiatives, from kick-off through execution, and ensure alignment among all impacted parties Demonstrable leadership capabilities, with the capacity to use data to influence key stakeholders and senior leadership on recommendations Excellent oral and written communication skills with ability to convey complex topics or strategies to stakeholders and leadership A “roll up your sleeves” mindset and ability to get work done independently when required Strong interpersonal skills and an approachable style Sound judgment and decision-making ability Ability to accelerate impact and champion change Effective collaboration with a wide range of constituencies in a diverse community You will: Lead high impact initiatives that align with and move us towards our core business objectives Facilitate market deep dives to understand local market dynamics, gain alignment around historical performance, identify opportunity areas, and develop and track credible action plans Perform extensive quantitative analysis by leveraging internal and external data to identify key insights and trends, and collaborate with field leaders to develop and execute related strategies to win in market Build and apply knowledge of market trends to identify, engage and develop partner solutions to optimize delivery of capability needs required to win in market Identify new and unique growth opportunities and develop strategies to execute on those opportunities Build credible relationships with business stakeholders and influence leaders to effect change and accomplish business outcomes Work collaboratively with Product, Distribution, Pricing, and Underwriting, amongst others, to capitalize on and enhance offers that tap into high growth segments of the market Demonstrate excellent communication skills, both written and oral, communicate effectively with key stakeholders (internal and external), business partners, and other audiences through various communication methods Location and Travel: This is a hybrid position requiring three days a week in a Guardian Office. Job Description: The Reviewing Dentist for appeal claims is a licensed dental professional who completes clinical reviews of dental claim appeals to determine benefit assignment in accordance with Guardian clinical guideline criteria. The role focuses on benefit determination (not treatment recommendations) and supports consistent, fair application of dental policy. Responsibilities also include assigned peer‑to‑peer and provider outreach calls. This position reports to the Assistant Dental Director. Schedule: 24 hours/week. Key Responsibilities Clinical Appeals Review Independently review appealed claims and clinical documentation. Apply clinical criteria, dental policy, and benchmarks to ensure consistent, defensible decisions. Document clear clinical rationale to support decisions, compliance, and audits. Escalate cases as needed to the Dental Director/leadership. Communicate openly, demonstrate self-awareness, and incorporate feedback. Participate in monthly updates, quarterly IRR exercises, and required calibration sessions. Support implementation of new review policies/criteria as directed. Meet production, accuracy, and performance standards. Peer‑to‑Peer Communications Conduct peer‑to‑peer communications with treating providers (phone and/or written) to explain documentation‑based clinical determinations. Communicate decisions clearly and professionally, with a focus on transparency and provider understanding. Support positive provider relationships through thoughtful, clinically grounded communication. Conduct provider outreach calls to request missing documentation and/or communicate review outcomes. Vendor & Cross‑Functional Collaboration Partner, as needed, with an external clinical review vendor to align documentation standards, clinical interpretation, and review quality. Collaborate with Dental Claims, Network, Product, Compliance, and Quality partners to support clinical operations. Provide clinical input on appeal trends, documentation gaps, and process improvement opportunities. Qualifications DDS or DMD from an accredited dental school. Active, unrestricted dental license in at least one U.S. state. Minimum 5+ years of clinical dental experience. Prior experience in dental claims clinical review. Utilization review, clinical appeals, managed care, or similar experience preferred. Ability to render sound clinical decisions based on submitted documentation, clinical criteria, and industry standards. Strong written and verbal communication skills, including peer‑to‑peer communications via phone and email. This individual will support all facets of incentive compensation strategy and design - including designing, modeling, goal setting, analysis, and communicating incentive compensation for our internal and external Group Sales Distribution team. Job Description: Strategic Data Initiative Lead Enterprise Data Solutions & Operations (EDSO), Guardian Digital & Technology (D&T) Job Description The Strategic Data Initiative Lead  is a critical role responsible for orchestrating and executing the company’s most pivotal, large-scale transformation initiatives. This individual will operate at the nexus of strategy and execution, responsible for not only the meticulous, hands-on delivery of complex programs but also for crafting and communicating critical program level narratives related to status, budget and progress to C-suite executives and senior leaders. ​ Reporting to the Head of Strategic Data Initiatives this individual will guide cross-functional teams in a matrixed environment to deliver transformational changes that reshape our business processes, technology platforms, and competitive positioning. The ideal candidate is a seasoned leader with a proven history of managing ambiguous, high-impact data transformation programs from conception to completion within a large complex enterprise, preferably in an insurance context. You will ​​Lead the end-to-end execution of large-scale data transformation programs ensuring they are delivered on-scope, on-time, and on-budget Facilitate collaboration between disparate business, technology, and operational teams to ensure seamless integration and alignment. Establish and manage robust program governance, including risk and dependency management, issue resolution, resource planning, and detailed project milestone tracking. ​Directly manage program-level budgets, forecasting, and resource allocation to ensure efficient use of capital and headcount. ​​Develop and articulate a compelling strategic narratives for approaches to transformation initiatives, translating complex program details into clear, concise, and persuasive stories for an executive audience. ​Create high-impact presentation materials (decks) and communication artifacts to rally C-suite and senior leadership around the approach to delivery of a program's vision, as well as reporting on progress. ​Serve as a partner to critical stakeholders, proactively managing expectations and ensuring continuous alignment through rigorous communications and transparency in delivery processes. Form relationships with critical business and technology stakeholders and establish a trusted working relationship. ​You Have ​​Bachelor's degree in Business, Technology, or a related field. ​8+ years of progressive experience in program management, with a demonstrated track record of successfully leading enterprise-wide business or digital transformation initiatives. ​Proven experience leading large-scale programs in a complex and regulated industry (e.g., Financial Services, Banking, Healthcare). ​Exceptional executive-level communication, presentation, and storytelling skills, with the ability to distill complexity into clarity and influence senior decision-makers. ​Mastery of leading through influence in a highly matrixed organization, with a demonstrated ability to build consensus and drive results without direct authority. Preferred: ​Direct experience with Life Insurance, Disability Insurance, and Dental Insurance ​MBA or other advanced degree. Measures of Success: ​Performance in this role will be evaluated on a balanced set of outcomes, including: ​Business Value Realization: Achievement of defined business outcomes and strategic goals (e.g., market launch of new products, realization of expense efficiencies, successful platform modernization). ​Program Delivery Excellence: Consistent on-time and on-budget delivery of major program milestones and deliverables. ​Stakeholder Satisfaction & Organizational Adoption: Positive feedback from executive sponsors and key stakeholders, coupled with the successful adoption and embedding of new processes, technologies, and ways of working across the organization. Job Description: Job Description: Job Description: Consultant – IT (Java, Spring Boot, Microservices, AWS) Role Summary We are looking for an experienced technical lead on the java backend/full stack development side who has a proven track record of leading high‑performance engineering teams and delivering scalable, production‑ready systems with speed and quality. This role requires strong technical depth, leadership capabilities, and effective collaboration with business and technology stakeholders across the organization. Education & Experience Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field. 12+ years of experience as a backend/J2EE software engineer, with significant experience in Java-based enterprise applications. Proven experience leading development teams independently in Agile environments. Key Responsibilities Lead an Agile development team as part of an Agile Release Train (ART), ensuring timely and high-quality delivery. Design, develop, and maintain robust, scalable, and production-ready backend systems using Java and Spring Boot-based microservices. Ensure first-time-right delivery by driving engineering best practices, quality standards, and thorough review processes. Engineer application integrations using REST and SOAP APIs, messaging systems, and event-driven architectures. Work closely with Business and Product teams and Solution Architects to ensure alignment with the product roadmap and architectural blueprints. Mentor, coach, and guide engineers and analysts; assign work, enforce standards, and perform detailed code reviews. Lead troubleshooting and triage of end-to-end system integration issues across applications, middleware, and infrastructure layers. Collaborate with Enterprise Architecture, Platform, Security, and Production Support teams to deliver customer-centric solutions. Participate in production support activities when required, including issue analysis and coordination to ensure timely resolution. Demonstrate flexibility to occasionally align with US business hours for stakeholder interactions, releases, and critical production needs. Technical Skills & Expertise Backend & Integration Strong proficiency in Java and Spring Boot for microservices development. Hands-on experience designing and implementing RESTful and SOAP APIs. Experience with API design standards using OpenAPI/Swagger specifications. Solid understanding of data-driven design, enterprise integration patterns, and event-driven architectures. Experience working with TIBCO BW. Experience with messaging and event platforms such as ActiveMQ, IBM MQ, or similar technologies. Cloud & DevOps Experience working with AWS services such as S3, SQS, SNS, Lambda, Redis (ElastiCache), and OpenSearch. Basic working knowledge of containerization and orchestration platforms including Docker, ECS, and EKS. Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and build tools such as Jenkins, Maven, Git etc. Understanding of infrastructure and cloud monitoring using AWS CloudWatch and Splunk. Data & Caching Strong experience with relational databases such as PostgreSQL. Working knowledge of NoSQL databases. Experience leveraging caching technologies such as Redis to improve application performance and scalability. Testing & Quality Experience writing unit and integration tests using frameworks such as JUnit. Good to have exposure to Python for scripting, tooling, or automation use cases. Strong focus on code quality, test coverage, and production readiness. Security & Observability Experience with API security mechanisms including OAuth 2.0, JWT, and token-based access controls. Familiarity with logging and monitoring tools such as Splunk, AppDynamics, and similar observability platforms. Additional Experience (Good to Have) Exposure to enterprise integration frameworks such as Apache Camel, MuleSoft, Tibco BusinessWorks, or similar. Experience working with rules engines such as Drools, OpenL, Blaze, or equivalent. Understanding of Agile and SAFe methodologies. AWS Cloud certifications and SAFe Agile certifications are preferred. Skills & Attributes Strong foundation in computer science fundamentals and SDLC concepts. Ability to lead teams independently and take ownership of delivery outcomes. Excellent problem-solving, analytical, and troubleshooting skills. Strong stakeholder management and communication skills. Collaborative mindset with the ability to work across distributed and cross-functional teams. Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, enterprise-scale environment. Job Description: POSITION OVERVIEW:  Product Owner, Group Benefits Technology About Guardian Every day, Guardian helps our 29 million customers realize their dreams through a range of insurance and financial products and services. Our Purpose, to inspire well-being, guides our dedication to the colleagues, consumers, and communities we serve. We know that people count, and we go above and beyond to prepare them for the life they want to live, focusing on their overall well-being — mind, body, and wallet. As one of the largest mutual insurance companies, we put our customers first. Behind every bright future is a Guardian.  Learn more about Guardian at guardianlife.com. About the Opportunity You will be a key contributor to the product vision, capturing and prioritizing requirements , creating a roadmap, and facilitating successful product implementation in Group Business team. You will work closely with our user experience, development, quality assurance teams in addition to other internal stakeholders to deliver new capabilities to both internal and external customers. You will: Serve as product and project owner on several high impact deliverables. Collaborate with internal stakeholders, including cross-functional teams and business units, to understand their needs, translate them into technical solutions / user stories, and set expectations. Partner with technology teams to meet the overall release plan at the project and team level, understand blockers/limitations and their impact on the product and relevance to end users. Set priorities, create a roadmap, and establish clear objectives for the technology team responsible for building and enhancing the product. Create release and launch plans in coordination with the Business and Engineering teams. Grooming and prioritization of the backlog. Monitor and analyze key performance indicators (KPIs) and user feedback to identify opportunities for improvement and drive continuous enhancements (feedback loops). Stay up to date with industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices related to insurance and user experience. Filter requests—pushing back when necessary—and prioritize requests in order to keep the team focused on delivering impactful solutions. You Are: Outcome oriented:  Biased toward action and delivering quantifiable results. Passion for driving continuous improvement and delivering value to the organization. You are p roactive/ self-driven and can operate with minimal supervision Data Driven and Customer Focused: Detail-oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving abilities that balances quantitative and qualitative inputs to optimize the user experience and/or business objectives. Team Player: You enjoy working with diverse people and driving the team toward a common goal. You value establishing long-lasting relationships. Intellectually curious: You have an appetite to learn new things and are comfortable speaking up and challenging the status quo. You are comfortable with ambiguity and can easily adapt to changing priorities, manage multiple tasks, and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment. You Have: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field, or equivalent experience. At least 8-10 years of digital or technical experience, as a Product Owner, Product Manager, or in a similar role, a plus if in a technical or regulated environment. At least 3 years of Agile experience or a demonstrable understanding of Agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban, SAFE, etc.) Familiarity with Jira or similar backlog management tools and software development lifecycle methodologies Experience working on both customer facing products as well as backend products (DBs and APIs) Knowledge of data privacy regulations, and security best practices is a plus. Ability to break down technical issues into easily digestible data points for stakeholder consumption. Excellent communication skills and a high level of emotional intelligence to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams and stakeholders. Demonstrated ability to initiate, define and deliver on strategic initiatives. History of working well in a group setting and proven ability to deliver results. Job Description: Job Description: Senior Lead Engineer – ESQA (Quality Engineering) Education & Experience B.Tech / BE / MCA in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field. 8–10 years of experience in software engineering with a strong focus on quality engineering, test automation, and enterprise application testing. Solid hands-on experience in Java, with the ability to contribute beyond test automation when required. Key Responsibilities & Essential Skills Lead quality engineering activities for one or more product areas, ensuring high standards of quality across the delivery lifecycle. Collaborate with product owners, business analysts, architects, and development leaders to understand business requirements, system design, and quality expectations. Provide technical leadership in Java-based test automation and supporting frameworks. Extensive hands-on experience with REST Assured for API automation and Playwright using TypeScript and/or Java. Own and evolve the test automation strategy, frameworks, standards, and best practices in alignment with application architecture and roadmap. Review requirements, user stories, and technical designs to identify test scenarios, edge cases, and quality risks early in the lifecycle. Design, review, and ensure effective execution of automated and manual test cases covering functional, regression, integration, and end-to-end scenarios. Lead sprint-level quality activities including story readiness, test coverage, regression analysis, and release validations. Guide and mentor team members in identifying regression scenarios and converting them into reliable and maintainable automation. Perform and oversee SDLC activities such as requirement analysis, impact analysis, test planning, and effort estimation. Ensure effective defect management, including clear defect reporting, prioritization, root cause analysis, and validation of fixes. Partner closely with development teams to improve testability, code quality, and overall engineering outcomes. Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and provide technical guidance in analyzing complex issues reported by business or production support teams. Participate in production support activities on a rotation basis when required, including incident analysis, root cause identification, and coordination with relevant teams. Demonstrate flexibility to occasionally align with US business hours to support releases, stakeholder interactions, and critical production needs. Ensure compliance with organizational quality standards, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices. Prepare and present test metrics, quality KPIs, and release readiness reports to stakeholders. Drive continuous improvement initiatives across quality processes, tools, and methodologies. Mentor and coach engineers across levels, promoting strong quality and engineering practices. Take ownership of delivery outcomes and proactively identify risks, dependencies, and improvement opportunities. Provide regular and effective status updates on progress, risks, and overall quality posture at the program or release level. Nice to Have Strong analytical, debugging, and problem-solving skills with a system-level thinking approach. Effective communication and stakeholder management skills. Experience leading quality efforts in large, complex Agile delivery environments. Prior exposure to insurance or other highly regulated enterprise domains. Job Description: Do you want to be the go-to expert for complex life underwriting decisions and help lead the adoption of next-generation tools? As an Underwriting Consultant, you will serve as a senior technical expert within the Life Underwriting practice for Financial Protection and Retirement Solutions. You will apply specialized expertise to adjudicate the most complex cases, troubleshoot escalations, and guide peers in leveraging modern underwriting technologies. This role ensures competitive, customer-centric, and compliant underwriting practices while aligning with our values of We do the right thing, We believe people count, We courageously shape our future together, and We go above and beyond for the people we serve. You are A recognized authority in life underwriting with the ability to interpret complex business issues and deliver sound, innovative solutions. You are comfortable adapting precedent and introducing approaches that improve efficiency and customer experience while maintaining favorable mortality results within company expectations. You are also a trusted resource for peers and leaders, providing guidance on challenging cases and the adoption of digital tools. You have Specialized depth and breadth of expertise in individual life underwriting, including authority on complex and high dollar cases. Proven ability to adopt and champion technology-enabled solutions, leveraging automated underwriting platforms and data-driven tools. Demonstrated experience working within reinsurance frameworks, including interpreting treaty language, understanding retention limits, applying automatic and facultative reinsurance rules, and collaborating with reinsurance partners. Familiarity with emerging technologies (Electronic Health Record (EHR), GenAI, wearable technology) and behavioral economics concepts. Familiarity with quality control programs (e.g., Random Holdouts (RHO), Post-Issue Audits (PIA)) and applying analytics for continuous improvement. Strong problem-solving and decision-making skills, able to adapt precedent and develop innovative approaches. Excellent communication and collaboration skills, capable of influencing peers and supporting leadership. You will Serve as one of the top technical underwriter on the team, adjudicating the most complex and high-value cases, reviewing appeals, reference, reinsurance submissions, ensuring decisions align with underwriting guidelines, mortality, morbidity, and product expectations. Provide expert judgment and guidance on escalated cases, troubleshooting complex issues for all levels of staff and management. Apply deep reinsurance expertise to ensure appropriate use of automatic and facultative reinsurance, evaluate treaty requirements, and partner with reinsurance underwriters as needed. Serve as a trusted underwriting partner to Distribution and Field leadership, delivering clear guidance, responsive support, and consultative expertise to drive case placement, strengthen relationships, and ensure a seamless experience for producers and customers. Maintain deep knowledge of underwriting guidelines, risk assessment methodologies, and regulatory requirements, ensuring compliance and consistency. Champion the adoption and effective use of modern underwriting tools and platforms, ensuring integration into daily workflows. Troubleshoot complex underwriting issues across the underwriting team, applying innovative solutions. Mentor, train, and guide underwriters in advanced case handling and digital tool utilization. Monitor industry trends and share insights to inform prudent business decisions. Collaborate with the entirety of the life underwriting team (e.g., managers, transformation specialists, etc.) to ensure successful implementation of transformation initiatives. Job Title: Head of UI Design, Engineering & Accessibility
Job Description: Position Overview Guardian is seeking a Head of UI Design, Engineering & Accessibility to lead the strategy, delivery, and governance of our enterprise design system and front‑end standards. This role owns UI as a platform capability—ensuring scalable, accessible, and high‑quality digital experiences across all web and mobile surfaces. You will lead multidisciplinary teams spanning UI design, UI engineering, and accessibility while partnering closely with Product, Engineering, and Mobile teams as Guardian evolves toward AI‑native digital experiences. You Will (Key Responsibilities) Own and evolve Guardian’s enterprise design system as a platform product, including design tokens, component libraries, pattern standards, roadmaps, versioning, and adoption metrics. Lead and develop a high‑performing team of UI designers, UI engineers, and accessibility specialists, with clear operating distinctions between platform delivery and governance. Define and enforce enterprise UI engineering standards, including framework conventions, implementation quality, and performance benchmarks across all digital web experiences. Establish and govern accessibility standards across all digital surfaces, ensuring WCAG compliance through audits, release gates, and continuous improvement. Serve as the primary interface between the design system and product delivery teams, defining contribution models and governing adoption and usage. Partner with Mobile Engineering to ensure consistency across web and native platforms through shared token architectures and aligned interaction standards. Drive a UI Engineering Center of Excellence, uplifting capabilities across product engineering teams through standards, reviews, and embedded consultation. Recruit, develop, and retain pivotal talent across UI design, engineering, and accessibility, aligning staffing to critical enterprise needs. Define and track success metrics such as component adoption, system coverage, accessibility compliance, and team velocity, reporting these as enterprise capability indicators. Contribute to Guardian’s AI‑native digital experience strategy by shaping conversation design standards, agentic UI patterns, and multimodal accessibility approaches. You Have (Key Qualifications) Extensive experience leading enterprise‑scale UI design systems and front‑end engineering platforms in complex organizations. Proven ability to lead multidisciplinary teams across design, engineering, and accessibility functions. Deep expertise in UI engineering standards, modern front‑end frameworks, and performance optimization. Strong command of digital accessibility standards, including WCAG compliance and governance models. Experience partnering with product, engineering, and mobile teams to drive adoption and consistency at scale. Demonstrated success building centers of excellence and influencing enterprise‑wide digital practices. Strategic mindset with the ability to define metrics, measure impact, and communicate outcomes to senior leadership. Passion for emerging digital experience models, including AI‑driven and conversational interfaces. Job Description: Lead, Cyber Security Assurance Testing The Lead, Cyber Security Assurance Testing is a working‑lead / “player‑coach role” within Guardian’s Cybersecurity Assurance organization. This role includes formal people management responsibility for a small number of senior engineers, with a focus on operational and tactical planning ,execution, and reporting, contributing to strategic planning, and accountable for project and program outcomes. The role is structured roughly as: ~60% leadership, coordination, and program execution ~40% hands‑on technical contribution and research This position leads through influence, technical credibility, and ownership. It is designed for an experienced security professional who demonstrates ambition, strong execution discipline, and the potential to grow into broader leadership over time, while remaining close to the work today. The role supports and helps evolve Guardian’s Application Security Testing, Ethical Hacking (Red Team), and Third‑Party Testing programs, working closely with Cyber Defense, application owners, and Guardian India Security Assurance. Key Responsibilities Lead and coordinate day ‑ to ‑ day execution of: Application Security Testing Ethical Hacking / Red Team activities Third‑party penetration testing engagements Lead the Improvement and evolution of AppSec and Red Team programs, increasing consistency, structure, and measurable outcomes. Own and maintain processes, procedures, playbooks, and documentation to ensure clarity, repeatability, and quality. Contribute to and execute against program planning artifacts, including Roadmaps, backlogs, and quarterly and annual goals Coordinate work across US And India Based assurance staff and third party testing vendors. Remain actively involved in application security testing and ethical hacking, providing technical guidance and quality assurance Participate selectively in: Targeted application security testing Rating & Validation of high‑risk or high‑impact findings Red‑team or adversarial exercises where appropriate Provide technical review and direction: Support security product evaluation, selection, proof‑of‑concepts, and implementation, ensuring tools are operationalized effectively. Communicate technical findings clearly and simply to non‑technical stakeholders. Translate testing results into risk‑based, actionable insights. Collaborate closely with Cyber Defense, application teams, and security stakeholders to ensure testing results lead to defensive improvements and remediation. Required Qualifications 5+ years of experience in application security testing, ethical hacking, or offensive security, with demonstrated leadership responsibility. Strong understanding of: Application security testing techniques Penetration testing vs. red team objectives Secure SDLC and risk-based testing Hands on technical experience validating vulnerabilities and testing application security controls. Experience improving or evolving existing security programs, rather than only building from scratch. Strong process orientation with proven ability to create practical, lightweight documentation. Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts in simple, business relevant terms. Demonstrated ambition and intent to grow into broader leadership scope over time. Bachelor’s degree in a related field or equivalent experience/certifications. Preferred Qualifications Experience in financial services or other large, regulated enterprise environments. Exposure to cloud‑native application security and modern CI/CD environments. Experience working with globally distributed teams (e.g., US and India). Familiarity with AppSec and Offensive Security tools. Job Description: Position Summary The Sales Enablement Field Communications Manager is responsible for enabling sales success through content strategy, clear leadership communications, and best‑in‑class enablement experiences. This role serves as the owner of the sales content ecosystem—anchored in Seismic—and as a key communications partner to sales and distribution leadership. This individual translates enterprise strategy, product and program updates, and go‑to‑market priorities into compelling, field‑ready messaging and tools that drive alignment, confidence, and action across the sales organization. The role sits within Field Marketing and partners closely with Sales Enablement, Product, Training, and Enterprise Communications. Define and maintain Seismic information architecture , navigation standards, and content taxonomy to optimize findability and adoption. Partner with Product Marketing, Field Marketing, and Sales Enablement to curate, prioritize, and govern sales content across the buyer journey. Lead the creation and maintenance of high‑impact enablement assets (playbooks, pitch decks, talk tracks, FAQs, leave‑behinds, conference hubs, etc.). Monitor usage, engagement, and feedback to continuously optimize content strategy and inform future enablement investments. Sales Leadership Communications Develop ongoing communications for the sales team including leadership announcements, product and service updates, broker enablement, FAQs and talking points. Translate complex initiatives into clear, concise, and field‑relevant narratives. Ensure sales representatives and client managers are equipped with consistent messaging and supporting materials to confidently communicate with clients and partners. Support key moments such as weekly newsletters, sales conferences and meetings with aligned messaging and enablement. Strategic Messaging & Alignment Create and maintain core messaging frameworks that align enterprise strategy with sales priorities. Ensure consistency across channels including Seismic, sales meetings, leadership communications, newsletters, and enablement campaigns. Proactively identify gaps, risks, and opportunities in sales messaging and enablement. Build strong relationships with sales leaders and field stakeholders to understand needs and influence adoption. Balance strategic thinking with hands‑on execution in a fast‑paced, deadline‑driven environment. You Have Bachelor’s degree required Proven in enablement : You bring 5+ years of experience in Sales Enablement, Field Marketing or Sales Training within SaaS, Fintech, or a similarly complex B2B environment Comfortable with enterprise sales : You understand long sales cycles, multiple stakeholders, and integrated multi-product deal journeys Credible and clear : You can stand in front of senior sellers, earn trust quickly, and turn strategy into practical execution Data-minded : You care about outcomes, not activity, and know how to use data to show what’s working and what’s not Strong operator : You can run initiatives end-to-end, balance priorities, and keep momentum without needing constant direction Tech-savvy : Demonstrated experience owning or heavily leveraging Seismic or similar sales content management platforms; Comfortable working with email service platforms and Microsoft Copilot. Job Description: We are seeking a strategic, collaborative, and innovative leader to serve as the Head of AI Enablement & Best Practices for Financial Protection & Retirement Solutions (FPRS). In this highly visible role, you will help democratize AI across FPRS by enabling broad, responsible adoption of readily available AI tools—including Microsoft Copilot, Copilot Studio, and other enterprise‑approved GenAI and low‑code / no‑code agentic AI tools that enable workflow automation. The focus of this role is to unlock day‑to‑day business value by making AI accessible, practical, and scalable for non‑technical users across FPRS. You will work closely with FPRS leaders and cross‑functional partners to ensure AI is applied in practical, repeatable, and scalable ways that improve day‑to‑day work, rather than remaining isolated to bespoke solutions fit for larger scale use cases or technical teams only. A successful candidate will partner with business, technology, and enablement teams to identify common needs, patterns, and adoption challenges related to AI usage across FPRS. You will translate approved AI capabilities into standards, playbooks, prompts, and training assets, and collaborate with Business leaders to help teams integrate AI into everyday workflows—while ensuring performance thresholds are met, alignment with governance, risk, and compliance expectations (including writing and maintaining SOPs, when required). This job will require a healthy mix of hands-on building (e.g., writing prompts, creating training materials and user guides, etc.) where it makes sense, and helping others develop their own solutions where appropriate, ultimately contributing to more proficiency across the business overtime. This role reports into the Head of Business Transformation & Program Mgmt. (FPRS). You will be working hand in hand with FPRS Product and Value Stream leaders, Enterprise AI Strategy & Transformation, Technology, Risk and Regulatory Controls, and HR and Learning teams. You will also play a key role in supporting and scaling the FPRS AI Champion program, accelerating peer‑to‑peer learning, and helping to drive higher velocity adoption of contemporary AI tools. Your work will span across: AI enablement and democratization – drive broad adoption of AI productivity tools (e.g., Microsoft Copilot, Copilot Studio, etc.) across FPRS by defining practical guidance, standard usage patterns, and guardrails that enable non‑technical users to apply AI confidently in their daily work Best practices, playbooks, and prompts – develop and maintain AI playbooks, reusable prompt libraries, and workflow examples; where appropriate, build and refine Copilot prompts that solve day‑to‑day business challenges while emphasizing reuse and scalability Use‑case pattern scaling – partner with Product, Operations, Underwriting, and Service teams to identify, document, and scale repeatable AI use‑case patterns across Life, Disability, and Retirement, helping teams move from experimentation to standardized ways of working AI literacy and workforce enablement – collaborate with HR and Learning partners to support role‑based AI literacy and adoption, integrating tools, prompts, and standards into broader change and learning efforts AI Champion program enablement – support and help evolve the FPRS AI Champion network by providing guidance, prompt libraries, and feedback loops that surface adoption barriers, success stories, and opportunities for scale Adoption insights and continuous improvement – analyze usage patterns, adoption trends, and qualitative feedback to identify friction points, inform improvements to enablement assets, and surface insights to FPRS leadership and enterprise partners Sustainability and risk reduction – define handoff and sustainability standards to ensure AI tools, prompts, and automations remain maintainable over time, reducing key‑person dependency and inconsistent AI usage Communication and alignment – communicate clearly with stakeholders across FPRS to share guidance, adoption insights, and enablement updates, fostering trust, transparency, and continuous learning You are: A practical, strategic problem‑solver who can translate emerging technology into simple, usable guidance that improves how people work day to day Knowledgeable about AI productivity tools and trends, with experience supporting technology adoption and change in regulated environments Highly collaborative, able to partner effectively across business, technology, risk, and enablement teams Skilled at influencing without formal authority; comfortable working across functions and levels of the organization Organized and adaptable, able to prioritize across multiple enablement initiatives and stakeholder needs An effective communicator who can explain complex ideas clearly and credibly to diverse audiences Curious and experimentation‑minded, with a strong bias toward learning, reuse, and continuous improvement You have: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience 8+ years of experience in roles related to business transformation, technology enablement, digital productivity, or operational improvement Demonstrated experience supporting adoption of tools or ways of working across complex organizations Experience creating playbooks, standards, training assets, or communities of practice that scale capability beyond individual contributors Strong proficiency with Microsoft 365 tools (PowerPoint, Excel, Word); familiarity with Copilot and GenAI tools strongly preferred You will: Have exposure to, and help shape, how AI is adopted across FPRS Develop deep understanding of FPRS businesses, workflows, and customer journeys Work closely with senior leaders while influencing outcomes through enablement rather than ownership Be coached and supported by experienced leaders while continuing to grow as an enterprise‑minded leader Salary Range: $152,290.00 - $250,195.00 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. The salary for this position ultimately will be determined based on the education, experience, knowledge, and abilities of the successful candidate. In addition to salary, this role may also be eligible for annual, sales, or other incentive compensation.
